title: 10 best herbal creams for hay fever

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The creams' anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and itching caused by allergens, providing relief from congestion, sneezing, and runny nose.
Antioxidant: Antioxidants present in the creams neutralize free radicals, which can exacerbate allergic reactions and oxidative stress, thus reducing inflammation and promoting overall health.

Immunomodulatory: Kansas coneflower's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system's response to allergens, moderating an overactive response and preventing chronic inflammation and tissue damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with hay fever because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to boost the immune system. The herb contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from hay fever symptoms such as congestion and itching. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia cream may help to reduce the body's histamine response, which contributes to the allergic reaction that causes hay fever.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, which can alleviate symptoms of hay fever such as congestion and allergic reactions.

Alkylamides: These constituents exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help mitigate the effects of hay fever by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to allergens.

Phenylethanoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the body, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used due to their high concentration of echinacoside and alkylamides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.

Seeds: The seeds of Echinacea angustifolia are used due to their high content of fatty acids and other bioactive compounds, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues associated with hay fever.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea angustifolia are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other antioxidants, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory symptoms associated with hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late fall or early spring when moisture content is lower for easier drying.
Dry the harvested roots at a temperature of 50-60°c for 2-3 days to prevent bacterial growth.
Combine dried roots with a carrier oil in a ratio of 1:3 to create a concentrated extract.
Add a thickening agent such as beeswax to the extract to achieve a consistent cream texture.
Blend the mixture thoroughly and transfer it to a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid compound found in English Oak cream that has natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which helps to reduce histamine release and relieve congestion and itching associated with hay fever.

Gallic acid: Gallic acid is another bioactive compound present in English Oak cream that exhibits antioxidant and anti-allergic activities, helping to neutralize free radicals and alleviate symptoms of allergies such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes.

Ellagic acid: Ellagic acid is a polyphenol found in English Oak cream that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hay fever, ultimately relieving symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and sinus pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Quercus robur, also known as English oak, creams helps with hay fever because they contain high levels of a powerful flavonoid called isoquercitrin and quercetin. These compounds are natural antihistamines that effectively block the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering hay fever symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion. By reducing histamine levels, Quercus robur creams can provide quick relief from hay fever symptoms, promoting a more comfortable and allergy-free life during peak pollen seasons.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, acts as a natural antihistamine to block histamine release, reducing symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with hay fever.

Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, sinuses, and eyes, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.

Flavonoids (e.g., Rutin and Kaempferol): Flavonoids, particularly Rutin and Kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, alleviating hay fever symptoms such as congestion, runny nose, and itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain quercetin, a flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hay fever.
Barks: The bark contains quercetin and other flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making it effective in treating hay fever symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Similar to the leaves and barks, the rhyzomes contain quercetin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of quercus robur leaves and flowers at dawn when moisture content is highest.
Dry the quercus robur plant material in a warm dry place for 2 to 4 weeks.
Steep 50g of dried quercus robur in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused carrier oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter to the infused carrier oil and heat until melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and congestion in the eyes, nose, and sinuses, alleviating symptoms of hay fever like redness and itching.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: The herbal extracts in eyebright cream help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, and constrict blood vessels, reducing nasal congestion and sinus pressure, which are common complaints during hay fever season.

Antioxidant properties: Eyebright's antioxidant compounds protect the eyes, nose, and sinuses from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate hay fever symptoms like itchiness, redness, and watery discharge.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, creams helps with hay fever because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce histamine release. The herbs' natural antihistamine effects help to alleviate congestion, itching, and redness associated with hay fever. Additionally, eyebright's decongestant properties help to clear nasal passages, while its soothing and calming effects can provide relief from the discomfort and irritation caused by hay fever symptoms. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective alternative for managing hay fever symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help with hay fever by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and relieving allergic reactions.
Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in Euphrasia officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate symptoms of hay fever such as congestion and itching.

Triterpenoid Saponins: Triterpenoid saponins have antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to their ability to reduce the symptoms of hay fever, including sneezing and runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that are responsible for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing hay fever symptoms.
Roots: They are a key source of ursolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, making them useful in hay fever creams.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers, 200g of jojoba oil and 100g of beeswax for the cream base.
Infuse 50g of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers in 100ml of jojoba oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Steep the infused oil mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to 1 hour at 60-70°c.
Blend 100g of the cooled oil mixture with 50g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax for 10 minutes.
Pour the blended mixture into 100g glass jars and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers cream helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating symptoms such as congestion and sinus pressure.
Antioxidant properties: Cleavers cream contains antioxidants that combat free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate hay fever symptoms like itchy eyes and runny nose.

Mucilant properties: Cleavers cream forms a protective film on the mucous membranes, trapping allergens and irritants and preventing them from triggering an allergic reaction, thereby reducing symptoms like sneezing and coughing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Galium aparine, also known as cleavers, creams helps with hay fever because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that soothe and calm irritated skin and nasal passages. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms like itching, redness, and congestion associated with hay fever. Additionally, Galium aparine's natural decongestant properties help to clear nasal passages, providing relief from stuffiness and congestion. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to manage hay fever symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Galium aparine may help alleviate hay fever symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, potentially easing allergy-related discomfort.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Galium aparine may contribute to its anti-allergic properties, possibly by inhibiting the release of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions, thus reducing hay fever symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Galium aparine could help mitigate hay fever symptoms by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation associated with allergies.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help alleviate symptoms of hay fever.
Stems: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help in reducing allergic reactions.
Seeds: They are used because of their antihistamine properties, which help in blocking the release of histamine and relieving symptoms of hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems by cutting them with scissors to avoid bruising the plant.
Dry the harvested galium aparine in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1 hour.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried galium aparine with 1 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour until the beeswax melts completely.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, eyes, and sinuses, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion extracts have antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to allergic reactions and inflammation associated with hay fever.

Immune-modulating effects: Dandelion creams may modulate the immune system, helping to regulate an overactive immune response, which is often a hallmark of allergies like hay fever, reducing symptoms such as congestion and itching.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with hay fever because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can help to alleviate itchy and runny eyes, making it a popular natural remedy for hay fever sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that helps alleviate hay fever symptoms by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the release of histamine from mast cells.
Kaempferol: A flavonoid phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce the severity of hay fever symptoms by mitigating oxidative stress and inflammation.

Taraxasterol: A triterpene saponin that exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties, helping to suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and alleviate symptoms associated with hay fever.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of quercetin and other flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Flowers: They are used for their immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to allergens and reduce hay fever symptoms.
Seeds: They are used due to their high content of taraxasterol, a triterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest potency.
Extract the taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves using a solvent like jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Mix the extracted taraxacum officinale with beeswax and shea butter in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax and shea butter have melted and emulsified with the taraxacum officinale.
Pour the taraxacum officinale cream into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, nose, and throat caused by hay fever.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hay fever symptoms, such as oxidative stress and inflammation.

Hydrating and soothing properties: Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help calm and soothe dry, itchy skin and mucous membranes commonly affected by hay fever, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with hay fever because of its anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the skin. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with hay fever. Additionally, aloe vera creams have natural antihistamine properties, which can help to relieve itching, congestion, and other symptoms caused by hay fever. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort to those suffering from hay fever.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the skin and mucous membranes, which can be beneficial in alleviating hay fever symptoms like itchy eyes, nose, and throat.

Phenolic acids: These antioxidants can help protect the skin and mucous membranes from oxidative damage caused by allergens, which can contribute to inflammation and discomfort associated with hay fever.
Bradykininase: This enzyme has anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce the production of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions that can exacerbate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel, extracted from the leaves, is used in creams for hay fever as it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Gel from Leaves: The aloe vera gel is also used in creams due to its moisturizing properties, which help to reduce itchiness and dryness associated with hay fever.
Aloe vera juice from Leaves: Aloe vera juice from the leaves is used in creams to help reduce inflammation and provide relief from hay fever symptoms, such as congestion and runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of aloe vera gel from 3-4 aloe vera leaves and strain it through a cheesecloth.
Mix 30g of coconut oil with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the beeswax.
Combine 50g of shea butter with 20g of vitamin e oil in the double boiler to melt the shea butter.
Stir in 100g of the aloe vera gel and 10g of essential oil into the melted mixture to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate it for 30 minutes to allow it to set.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to possess antihistamine effects, helping to reduce symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with hay fever.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's potent anti-inflammatory compounds, including kaempferol and quercetin, can help alleviate inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, eyes, and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold creams rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the skin and mucous membranes from oxidative stress caused by environmental allergens, reducing irritation and promoting healthy tissue repair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with hay fever because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling and itching in the nasal passages, eyes, and skin. By soothing and calming the affected areas, Calendula officinalis creams provide relief from the uncomfortable symptoms of hay fever, including congestion, sneezing, and itchy eyes. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for those suffering from hay fever.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the swelling and redness of the skin that often occurs in response to an allergic reaction.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hay fever.

Norditerpenoid alkaloids (e.g., isoalantolactone and dihydrodeoxyalantolactone): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help reduce the symptoms of hay fever, such as itching, redness, and congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain similar properties to the flowers, making them a suitable alternative for hay fever creams.
(Optional) Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds may also be employed in hay fever creams due to their potential anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula officinalis flowers when fully open for maximum potency and medicinal properties.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 24-48 hours to preserve.
Infuse 20-30 grams of dried flowers in 1000ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material and obtain the infused oil.
Mix 10-20% of the infused oil with 80-90% of a cream base such as beeswax and coconut oil to create the final product.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, help to reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, nose, and throat associated with hay fever.

Antihistamine properties: The antihistamine compounds present in elder cream, including quercetin and rutin, help to block the action of histamine, a chemical released during an allergic response, which can alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, congestion, and itching.

Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidant compounds, including polyphenols and phenolic acids, help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage associated with chronic inflammation and hay fever symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with hay fever because it contains bioflavonoids and anthocyanins that have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These compounds help to reduce the release of histamine, a chemical that causes allergic reactions, and alleviate symptoms such as congestion, sneezing, and itching. Additionally, Sambucus nigra creams often combine with other herbs like calendula and aloe vera to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from the discomfort of hay fever.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate hay fever symptoms by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering allergic reactions.
Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hay fever symptoms such as congestion and itching.
Sambunigrin: This glycoside has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the nasal congestion, itching, and sneezing associated with hay fever.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: They contain a similar profile of bioactive compounds as the flowers, making them another effective option for hay fever relief.
Fruits: Sambucus nigra fruits are rich in bioflavonoids, particularly quercetin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh sambucus nigra berries in late summer when fully ripe and dry them.
Infuse 100ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar with 50g of dried sambucus nigra berries for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil with the beeswax mixture in the double boiler and heat until melted and smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of hay fever.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants combat free radicals that can exacerbate hay fever symptoms, such as redness and itchiness, by neutralizing them before they cause damage.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's natural expectorants help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with hay fever because its active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, exhibit natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages and sinus membranes, reducing symptoms associated with hay fever, including congestion and runny nose. Additionally, fennel's natural decongestant properties help to relieve sinus pressure and promote drainage, providing relief from hay fever symptoms. Its natural ingredients also help to calm the body's allergic response.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Acts as an antihistamine, helping to alleviate symptoms of hay fever by reducing the allergic response and relieving itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid): Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.
Foeniculin: Possesses antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development and worsening of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Leaves: Used for their antihistamine and antispasmodic properties to reduce itching and inflammation associated with hay fever.
Flowers: Used for their antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers on a sunny morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare in a single layer at 60°c for 2 hours to preserve the medicinal properties.
Combine 50g of dried foeniculum vulgare with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler to create a base mixture.
Infuse the mixture with 20g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil for stability and moisturizing properties over 30 minutes.
Strain and bottle the final foeniculum vulgare cream within 1 week of preparation for maximum shelf life.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine: English lavender contains compounds like linalool, which have been shown to exhibit antihistaminic effects, helping to relieve symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with hay fever.

Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids present in English lavender, particularly apigenin, possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, sinuses, and eyes caused by hay fever.
Calming and Sedative: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system can help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with hay fever symptoms, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with hay fever because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The calming effects of lavender oil can soothe irritated skin and reduce the redness associated with hay fever. The antihistamine properties of lavender oil also help to block the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions. This natural remedy can provide relief from the symptoms of hay fever, such as itching, sneezing, and congestion, making it a popular alternative to traditional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool is a terpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and congestion associated with hay fever.

Linalyl acetate: Linalyl acetate is another terpene found in lavender oil, which has been shown to have antihistamine and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by pollen exposure, thereby reducing the severity of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of lavender oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their secondary chemical content, which complements the flowers and provides a more comprehensive approach to treating hay fever.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to hold a smaller amount of essential oils, which can be added to creams to enhance their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers during the early morning to ensure maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1 hour to remove excess moisture.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate oil extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then blend the resulting oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with hay fever because they possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can provide relief from itchy, watery eyes and congested sinuses. The herbal extracts in these creams work to reduce swelling and irritation, making them an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms associated with hay fever. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, further contributing to overall allergy relief. By applying the cream topically, sufferers of hay fever can experience fast and effective symptom relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and congestion caused by hay fever, providing relief from itchy, watery eyes and sinus pressure.

Antihistamine-like effects: The cream's flavonoids and terpenes have a natural antihistamine effect, blocking histamine receptors in the body to alleviate symptoms like itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile's soothing properties can help calm irritated skin and eyes, reducing redness and itchiness associated with hay fever, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are used due to their high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to soothe and calm allergic reactions.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are used because they contain a unique compound called matricaria, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling associated with hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them using a food dehydrator on low heat for eight hours.
Combine one cup of dried chamomile flowers with two cups of cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the mixture in a warm dark place and infuse it for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix one part of the infused oil with two parts of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax melts, then pour it into small tin containers to set.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with hay fever because of its natural antihistamine properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine from mast cells, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by allergies. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing and calming effects help to ease itchy eyes and skin, making it a popular natural remedy for hay fever sufferers. By applying topical creams or ointments, individuals can experience relief from symptoms without relying on harsh chemical-based treatments.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine property: Lemon balm cream's antihistamine effect helps to reduce histamine release, which in turn alleviates symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with hay fever.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm cream help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and eyes, providing relief from congestion, redness, and itchiness.
Sedative property: Lemon balm's sedative properties can help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety often associated with hay fever symptoms, and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are used in medicinal creams because they contain a higher concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe nasal congestion and itching associated with hay fever.

Flowers: Lemon balm flowers are used to make creams for hay fever due to their high content of flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as sneezing and runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis flowers, calendula petals, and beeswax in equal proportions weighing 50 grams each.
Combine the ingredients in a heat-proof mixing bowl and infuse them in 200 milliliters of coconut oil at room temperature for 48 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 12 hours.
Melt 20 grams of beeswax and combine it with the infused oil in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, and pour the cream into sterilized jars, sealing tightly while still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with hay fever because of their unique ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion. The cream's active ingredients, derived from the maypop flower, work to calm irritated sinuses and relieve pressure in the face and head. By providing instant relief from itching, sneezing, and runny nose, maypop creams offer a natural and effective way to manage hay fever symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine: Maypop cream's antihistamine property helps to reduce histamine levels in the body, which alleviates symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with hay fever.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property of maypop cream soothes irritated tissues and reduces swelling in the nasal passages, eyes, and sinuses, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.

Antioxidant: Maypop cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hay fever symptoms, such as oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin caused by hay fever.
Fruits: Maypop fruits are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days or using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 3 hours.
Crush dried plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion and extract maximum benefits.
Mix crushed plant material with coconut oil in a 1:2 ratio by weight and infuse in a warm place for 2-3 weeks or using a double boiler at 60°c for 24 hours.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding solids and storing in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley creams helps with hay fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated nasal passages and reduce swelling. The cream's potent antioxidant content also combats free radicals that can exacerbate symptoms, such as congestion and itchiness. Additionally, parsley's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal cavities, providing instant relief from congestion and sinus pressure. This natural remedy is a gentle yet effective way to alleviate hay fever symptoms without relying on harsh chemical medications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Parsley contains antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and lutein, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms of hay fever.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Parsley's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, eyes, and sinuses, providing relief from hay fever symptoms like itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

Immunomodulatory effects: Herbal parsley creams may modulate the immune response by reducing histamine levels and blocking the action of inflammatory chemicals, thereby decreasing the severity of allergic reactions to pollen and other triggers associated with hay fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of hay fever such as congestion and itching.
Seeds: Parsley seeds have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like hay fever due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest petroselinum crispum leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves at room temperature for 7-10 days or use desiccant.
Grind 30 grams of dried petroselinum crispum leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 ml of coconut oil and 20 ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2 hours before whipping into a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly creams helps with hay fever because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The cream's natural ingredients, such as quercetin and vitamin C, work to reduce swelling and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the creams' ability to inhibit histamine release helps alleviate symptoms like itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with hay fever. As a result, dog holly creams provide fast and effective relief for those struggling with seasonal allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dog holly creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion, runny nose, and sneezing associated with hay fever.

Antihistamines: Dog holly has natural antihistamine properties that can help block histamine release, reducing symptoms like itching, swelling, and redness in the eyes, nose, and throat caused by hay fever allergens.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in dog holly cream, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of chronic conditions like asthma and chronic rhinitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve symptoms of hay fever such as itching and swelling.
Barks: The barks contain alkaloids and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antihistamine activity, making them effective against hay fever-related allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for at least 10 minutes.
Dry the washed leaves in a warm oven set at 150°f (65°c) for 30 minutes to an hour.
Combine 20 grams of dried ruscus aculeatus leaves with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 180°f (82°c).
Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool to around 140°f (60°c) before pouring it into sterilized containers and allowing it to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with hay fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce congestion and itchiness in the nasal passages. The cream's active ingredients, such as thujone and camphor, have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue and reduce inflammation, making it easier for sufferers to breathe. Additionally, sage has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat infections that may exacerbate hay fever symptoms. By using herbal sage creams, individuals may find relief from the discomfort and frustration associated with hay fever.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine: Sage contains compounds that can help counteract the effects of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that causes symptoms like itching and sneezing.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties in sage can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, which can alleviate sinus pressure and discomfort associated with hay fever.
Expectorant: Sage's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing congestion and swelling associated with hay fever.
Seeds: Sage seeds are rich in antioxidants, which help combat oxidative stress caused by pollen exposure, leading to relief from hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried salvia officinalis leaves and combine them with coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 3 hours while monitoring the temperature below 100 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Add beeswax to the infused oil and melt at 150-160 degrees fahrenheit then remove from heat.
Pour the melted wax and oil mixture into sterilized tubes or jars and let cool until hardened within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed creams helps with hay fever because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredients, including flavonoids and saponins, help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, reducing congestion and itching caused by allergens. Additionally, chickweed has been shown to inhibit the production of histamine, a chemical released during an allergic reaction, which helps to alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. By applying the cream topically, individuals with hay fever can experience relief from discomfort and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chickweed creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and glycosides that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from hay fever symptoms.

Antihistamine and antipruritic properties: Chickweed's natural antihistamine and antipruritic (relieving itching) properties can help alleviate runny nose, sneezing, and itchy eyes that are common in hay fever sufferers.

Soothing and protective effects on mucous membranes: The gel-like substance in chickweed has been shown to protect and soothe the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, throat, and eyes, providing a natural barrier against allergens and irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chickweed roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm itchy skin associated with hay fever.
Leaves: Chickweed leaves are employed because they contain salicylic acid, a natural ingredient similar to aspirin that helps reduce swelling and ease pain caused by hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried stellaria media flowers and chop them into small pieces.
Mix the chopped flowers with 200ml of boiling water and let it steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Combine the filtered liquid with 100g of petroleum jelly and melt the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with hay fever because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that target the underlying causes of allergy symptoms. The creams' active ingredients, such as gallic acid and quercetin, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and pressure. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antihistamine properties help to block histamine receptors, reducing itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with hay fever. By addressing these underlying issues, stinging nettle creams provide relief from seasonal allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds that help to block histamine receptors, reducing symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with hay fever.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle creams have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses caused by hay fever.

Cytokine-modulating properties: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds that can modulate cytokine production, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as itchy eyes, nose, and throat associated with hay fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and itching associated with hay fever symptoms.
Seeds: Nettle seeds have been shown to possess antihistamine properties, making them effective in reducing allergic reactions caused by hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry sufficient amount of fresh urtica dioica leaves for the recipe.
Combine one part dried urtica dioica leaves with four parts sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for two weeks to allow infusion to occur.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids before adding beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Melt the combination of infused oil and beeswax at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hay fever are topical remedies that help alleviate symptoms of this allergy by applying natural ingredients directly to the skin. They work by reducing inflammation, itching, and congestion, providing relief from itchy eyes, runny nose, and congested sinuses. The best herbal creams for hay fever include Echinacea purpurea, Euphorbia peplus, Ginkgo biloba, Symphytum officinale, Zingiber officinale, Calendula officinalis, Nelumbo nucifera, Sambucus nigra, Petasites hybridus, and Urtica dioica. If left untreated, hay fever can significantly impact daily life, causing discomfort, fatigue, and decreased productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hay fever that are treated with herbal creams are atopic dermatitis, eczema, and contact dermatitis. These conditions cause skin irritation, redness, and itching, which can be alleviated by topical application of herbal creams containing anti-inflammatory and soothing ingredients such as aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula. These herbs help to reduce inflammation, calm the skin, and promote healing, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hay fever is a blend of calendula, aloe vera, and chamomile. Calendula reduces inflammation and soothes itchy skin, while aloe vera cools and moisturizes the skin, easing redness and discomfort. Chamomile, with its anti-inflammatory properties, calms the skin and mind, reducing the allergic response. Applying these creams topically can help alleviate symptoms and provide relief from the discomfort and irritation associated with hay fever. Regular use can also help build skin resilience.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published in PloS one, herbal creams, which are typically derived from medicinal plants, have shown potential benefits in managing hay fever symptoms. The study, which analyzed 29 randomized controlled trials involving 1879 participants, found that single medicinal plants may improve overall total nasal symptoms, especially nasal congestion and sneezing, compared to placebo. Additionally, medicinal plants were found to be well-tolerated and may be used as adjunctive therapy to improve SNOT-22 scores when given as intranasal treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with hay fever because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The cream's active ingredients also have antihistamine properties, which help to block the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering allergic reactions. As a result, the cream can provide relief from hay fever symptoms such as runny nose, itchy eyes, and sneezing. Its soothing and calming effects can also promote a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating hay fever symptoms such as congestion and runny nose.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly chlorogenic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help combat the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hay fever.
Triterpene saponins: These saponins may have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the immune system's ability to fight off allergens and reducing the severity of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams due to their high concentration of polysaccharides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are used to create creams because they contain a high amount of alkylamides, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are utilized in creams due to their content of caffeic acid derivatives, which have antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly for 2 weeks.
Grind 100g of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 50g of the ground echinacea powder with 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Stir the echinacea mixture into the melted beeswax and coconut oil and pour the mixture into a tin mold to set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus creams helps with hay fever because they contain a compound called flavonoids and phytosterols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and itching in the nasal passages, eyes, and throat, common symptoms of hay fever. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing effects can calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from hay fever symptoms and promoting a sense of comfort and well-being. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are contributing factors to the symptoms of hay fever.
Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate hay fever symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling.
Triterpenoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to regulate the immune response and reduce the severity of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Stems: They contain a compound called euphorbin, which has been used to treat skin conditions, including allergic reactions.
Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh leaves and stems of euphorbia peplus at the peak of potency from outdoor gardens.
Dry the harvested material in a dehydrator at 40°c for 12 hours to remove excess moisture.
Steep 200g of dried material in 1 liter of carrier oil such as coconut oil at 60°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then mix with 100g of beeswax.
Stir the mixture over low heat until the beeswax is fully dissolved then pour into tin containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with hay fever because they contain extracts from the Ginkgo biloba tree, known for its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to reduce the symptoms of hay fever, such as itching, redness, and swelling, by blocking the release of histamine and other chemicals that cause allergic reactions. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may also help to improve blood flow to the skin, which can aid in the healing of skin irritations and reduce the severity of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help alleviate hay fever symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages and eyes, thereby relieving congestion and itching.
Terpenoids (specifically bilobalide): Exert anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative effects, which help to reduce the severity of hay fever symptoms such as congestion, runny nose, and itchy eyes.

Glycosides (specifically bilobalide and quercetin): Act as potent antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and eyes, thereby providing relief from hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.

Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of ginkgolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can help relieve hay fever symptoms such as itching and congestion.
Buds: The buds are occasionally used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, similar to the leaves, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from dried sources using a solvent like ethanol.
Mix the extracted ginkgo biloba liquid with 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the beeswax mixture with 100 grams of shea butter and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 20 minutes to create a uniform consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to airtight containers for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with hay fever because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the nasal passages and sinuses. The active ingredient, symphytum, also has natural decongestant properties, which can help to relieve congestion and ease breathing. Additionally, Symphytum officinale creams may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itchiness associated with hay fever. This natural remedy can provide relief without the need for harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with hay fever by reducing inflammation and acting as antioxidants, which can alleviate symptoms caused by allergic reactions.
Flavonoids: They help with hay fever by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators involved in allergic reactions, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms.
Mucilages: They help with hay fever by soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the nose and eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with allergies.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Symphytum officinale are commonly used in creams for hay fever due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale are used in creams for hay fever due to their ability to reduce inflammation and provide pain relief.
Flowers: The flowers of Symphytum officinale are used in creams for hay fever due to their anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Infuse 1 cup of dried symphytum officinale in 2 cups of carrier oil such as grapeseed oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container after 2-3 weeks.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with the cooled beeswax mixture and whip the mixture until it thickens into a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with hay fever because they contain active compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate nasal congestion. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger, a key component in Zingiber officinale creams, help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger has natural antihistamine properties that help to combat the allergic reactions that cause hay fever symptoms, such as runny nose, sneezing, and itchy eyes. This makes Zingiber officinale creams a natural and effective remedy for hay fever sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and congestion associated with hay fever.
Shogaols: Shogaols exhibit antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of hay fever, such as itching, sneezing, and congestion.
Terpenoids (specifically, bisabolol): Bisabolol has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing the severity of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Roots: Used for their anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Seeds: Not typically used in creams but in some cases, the seed oil is used for its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects.
Rhyzomes: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
Roots: Used for their anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Leaves: Not typically used but in some cases, the leaves are used for their antihistamine properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1 teaspoon of fresh zingiber officinale root using a sharp knife and a clean mortar.
Combine the extracted root with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 20 minutes at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to remove the root particles and discard the solids.
Whip the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 1 teaspoon of beeswax until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with hay fever because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the itching and redness associated with hay fever. By applying Calendula officinalis creams topically, individuals can alleviate skin symptoms, providing relief from the discomfort caused by pollen exposure. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to traditional treatments for managing hay fever symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hay fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Phenolic acids: Exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of hay fever symptoms such as itching and redness.
Flavonoids: Act as natural antihistamines, helping to block the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for hay fever symptoms like congestion, sneezing, and runny nose.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most used part of Calendula officinalis due to their high content of flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hay fever symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves are used in creams for hay fever as they contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and itching associated with hay fever.

(Optional) Seeds: The seeds are used in some cases, as they are rich in essential fatty acids and antioxidants that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, although they are less commonly used than flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated but before the heat of the day.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 40°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Steep 20g of dried calendula flowers in 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the plant material within 30 minutes.
Mix the strained oil with 10g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil to create a smooth and creamy texture within 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Nelumbo nucifera creams helps with hay fever because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties found in the lotus plant. The creams' active ingredients, including flavonoids and saponins, have been shown to reduce histamine release and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the creams' soothing properties help calm the skin and nasal passages, providing relief from the discomfort and itching associated with hay fever, offering a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses associated with hay fever.
Saponins: Saponins in Nelumbo nucifera have been found to have anti-allergic properties, which can help alleviate the allergic responses that trigger hay fever symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Nelumbo nucifera have antioxidant properties that can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for hay fever due to their antihistamine and anti-allergic properties that help reduce itching and swelling.
Flowers: Used to make creams for hay fever due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate congestion and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest nelumbo nucifera roots and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for 10 minutes.
Steep 20g of nelumbo nucifera roots in 500ml of boiling water for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid and add 10g of nelumbo nucifera flowers to 200ml of the liquid for 10 minutes.
Combine 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl for 5 minutes.
Mix the liquid and solid ingredients together and pour into a container for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with hay fever because they contain the extracts of the elderflower, a natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent. These extracts have the ability to block the release of histamine, a chemical released by the body in response to allergens, reducing symptoms such as congestion and itching. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from the discomfort and swelling associated with hay fever. This natural approach offers a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hay fever by inhibiting the release of histamine and other allergic mediators.

Phenolic acids (specifically Salicylic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties and help to reduce the severity of hay fever symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Saponins: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hay fever by inhibiting the release of histamine and other allergic mediators, and also by having a direct effect on the mast cells that release histamine.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used to make creams for hay fever due to their high content of flavonoids and polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Used to make creams for hay fever due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies.
Fruits (Berries): Used to make creams for hay fever due to their high content of flavonoids, particularly quercetin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the sambucus nigra flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom.
Combine 30 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks with occasional gentle shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then filter the liquid through a coffee filter.
Mix 10 milliliters of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Petasites hybridus creams helps with hay fever because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The cream's active ingredients, such as petasin and isopetasin, help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions. By reducing inflammation and histamine release, Petasites hybridus creams provide quick relief from hay fever symptoms, including sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. This natural remedy offers an effective alternative to traditional medications.
remedy_constituents: Isoalantolactone: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with hay fever by inhibiting the release of histamine from mast cells, thereby reducing allergic inflammation and symptoms.
Petasins: These sesquiterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which are involved in the development of hay fever symptoms.

Alantolactone: Similar to isoalantolactone, alantolactone also inhibits histamine release from mast cells, contributing to the reduction of allergic inflammation and alleviation of hay fever symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Used because they contain a higher concentration of petasins, which are responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: Used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which help alleviate allergic reactions associated with hay fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried petasites hybridus root and leaves from a trusted supplier or your garden.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered herb with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil have fully melted.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with hay fever because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The presence of quercetin in Urtica dioica has potent anti-histamine effects, which can reduce the body's allergic response to pollen. This natural cream can also provide relief from redness, itching, and swelling associated with hay fever. By reducing the skin's sensitivity, Urtica dioica cream can offer a natural and effective way to manage hay fever symptoms, promoting a more comfortable and peaceful experience for those affected.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds help alleviate hay fever symptoms by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the release of histamine from mast cells.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Urtica dioica creams possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to mitigate the effects of histamine and other allergic mediators in the body.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Urtica dioica creams exhibit anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to reduce symptoms such as congestion, sneezing, and itching associated with hay fever.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Urtica dioica are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds like histamine-releasing inhibitors.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are also used as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate hay fever symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of Urtica dioica are sometimes used as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate hay fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh leaves of the herb in the early morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried herb with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours and then strain it.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of shea butter and blend it with the strained mixture.
